summary: Iteration 310 — Verified the Stock-by-SKU carton double-count fix in routes/distributors.py :: get_stock_dashboard :: _item_crates. Backend + Frontend both PASS. Backend curl and pytest confirm that for distributor bb12d90e-4d33-4890-ac5f-17573c551b5c (tenant nyla-air-water): Nyla – 660 ml / Sparkling has stock_delivered=24 (NOT 288 → double-count bug is dead); Nyla – 330 ml / Silver has stock_pending_out=15 (well below the 144 that a double-count would produce, and >=12 which confirms the DRAFT reservation is still being counted). Received magnitudes match the expected order-of-magnitude (600 Silver ≈1857, 330 Silver=166, Sparkling=83, 660 Gold=24). Available (on-hand − reserved) is non-negative for every SKU. Legacy code path (packaging_units>1 & no packages field → quantity × packaging_units) was verified by a direct helper-replica unit test; the plain-line path (no packaging_units, no packages → quantity as-is) was also verified. Frontend: logged in as CEO, opened /distributors/bb12d90e-…/ (Brian, DIST-0003), clicked the Stock Dashboard tab (data-testid stock-dashboard-tab) and the sku-stock-table renders the same values as the API — Sparkling DELIVERED = 24, 330 Silver RESERVED = 15, 600 Silver 1,857 / 64 / 2 / 1,791. The Bottles / Default Crates toggle (data-testid stock-unit-toggle with stock-unit-bottles / stock-unit-crates) works — Sparkling stays at 24 delivered in both modes (correct, its base UOM is Bottle with no default crate; the table just annotates '≈ 59 Bottle' next to the available cell). Bottom Totals row shows 2,130 received / 93 delivered / 17 reserved / 2,020 available across 4 SKUs which is internally consistent with the per-row sums.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/distributors.py get_stock_dashboard is now ~1350 lines long and defines _item_crates as a closure. It is applied to shipment_items, delivery_items, pending_items, and recent_items — but the two other item shapes on the same endpoint (transfer_in_by_sku / transfer_out_by_sku around line 8733) use a separate qty × units_per_package formula and do NOT participate in the new `packages` disambiguation. If distributor_stock_transfers rows ever start carrying `packages`, this side path would still multiply and double-count. Consider unifying to a single helper that takes any of {quantity, packaging_units, packages, units_per_package} and returns base units — or, at minimum, adding a comment noting the transfer path is intentionally different.

The projection now includes `packages` in four separate `.find(..., {..., 'packages': 1})` calls (~lines 8574, 8762, 8786, 8814). Any future line-item field the aggregation needs would repeat this pattern. Extract a module-level constant LINE_ITEM_PROJECTION = {'_id': 0, 'sku_id': 1, 'sku_name': 1, 'quantity': 1, 'packaging_units': 1, 'packages': 1} and reuse to prevent one of the four getting out of sync again.

The docstring on _item_crates is excellent (documents both storage models). Consider surfacing the same explanation on the DB write path (wherever the delivery/shipment line item is persisted) so authors know which model to use — the bug's root cause was silently switching from OLD to NEW model without updating the aggregator.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — Stock Dashboard tab renders, sku-stock-table shows Sparkling DELIVERED=24, unit toggle Bottles↔Crates works, all data-testids present

Login flow: fill email + password + press Enter on the password field (or click the 'Sign In' button — it is a plain <button> with no type=submit, so a form submit via the email/password Enter key is what actually posts).
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing distributor bb12d90e-4d33-4890-ac5f-17573c551b5c (Brian) and its existing shipment/delivery data for the 4 SKUs (600 Silver, 330 Silver, 660 Sparkling, 660 Gold).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fix is landed and working. The _item_crates helper now branches on the `packages` field. Regression test lives at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_310_stock_dashboard_carton_doublecount.py and is CI-friendly (loads REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from env). Note: pytest requires REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL to be exported before running because the module reads it at import time — run with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL /app/frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) pytest backend/tests/test_iteration_310_...py`. If the same double-count pattern surfaces on any other endpoint that aggregates distributor_delivery_items / distributor_shipment_items, look for `quantity * packaging_units` without a `packages` guard.
rca of the issue: Root cause (as documented by main agent and now verified): two storage models for delivery/shipment line items coexist in production. NEW-model rows persist `quantity` as the base-unit (bottle) total and also carry `packages` × `packaging_units`. OLD-model rows persist `quantity` as the number of packages and rely on `packaging_units` to expand to bottles. The dashboard's original _item_crates blindly did `quantity * packaging_units`, which for NEW-model rows means base-units × packaging-units, i.e. a 150-bottle line stored as (quantity=150, packages=10, packaging_units=15) came out as 2,250 instead of 150. Fix: branch on the presence of a positive `packages` field — if set, `quantity` is already the base total, so return it unchanged; if unset (or 0) and `packaging_units > 1`, fall back to the legacy multiplication; otherwise return quantity as-is. Same helper is now used in the shipment, delivery, pending (reserved), and weekly-average paths, so the four aggregations stay in lock-step. Verification for this iteration used the equivalent NEW-model rows on distributor bb12d90e (the production Anaia Cafe rows are not in preview): Sparkling has 2 completed deliveries of qty=12/pu=12/pk=1, expected 24, got 24 ✔; Silver 330 has a DRAFT of qty=12/pu=12/pk=1 which is counted as 12 (total pending 15 including a 3-unit hop, not 144) ✔.
